mechanism_narrative: Sustained declines in the federal funds rate reduce the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly boosting equity valuations over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 8.0 percent, it signals a tightening labor market that historically precedes wage-driven inflation and Fed tightening, compressing equity multiples over the next quarter.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day forward returns in more than 60 percent of triggered instances despite the negative signal, or Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous surge in industrial production and residential housing starts indicates broad-based economic acceleration that historically overheats demand, leading to margin compression and negative equity returns over the following month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative returns when both series rise concurrently, or the interaction term in a multivariate regression shows a p-value greater than 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-inflation environment, rising real activity forces the Fed to maintain restrictive policy, which historically drags down equity valuations over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The negative predictive power vanishes when inflation is below 2.5 percent, or forward returns are statistically indistinguishable from zero during high-inflation episodes.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent month-over-month growth in retail and food services sales reflects resilient consumer spending, which directly supports corporate revenue growth and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 1-year rolling window, or forward returns do not exhibit a positive skew during consecutive months of RSAFS growth.

mechanism_narrative: A break in industrial production above 95.0 indicates capacity utilization has reached sustainable maximums, historically leading to supply-side bottlenecks that erode profit margins and depress equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are not significantly negative when INDPRO exceeds 95.0, or the signal predictive power drops below 50 percent accuracy in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: When payroll employment growth accelerates while the federal funds rate remains near zero, it signals a labor-driven recovery that historically triggers premature monetary tightening, causing equity volatility and negative returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative returns when PAYEMS rises and FEDFUNDS stays flat, or the interaction coefficient in a panel regression is statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of housing market contraction, rising unemployment historically amplifies demand destruction, leading to sharp equity drawdowns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: The negative predictive power disappears when HOUST stabilizes, or forward returns are positive in more than 40 percent of triggered instances.
